verdict_reasoning: B2M (beta-2-microglobulin) is the non-covalent light chain subunit of MHC class I heterotrimers. It has no transmembrane domain of its own, but is assembled with the MHC class I alpha-heavy chain (which carries the TM domain) in the ER and co-trafficked as a stable complex to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane of virtually all nucleated cells. This is textbook 'stable_complex_partner' surface biology: intracellularly assembled, constitutively co-trafficked, and stably present on the cell surface. B2M also partners with CD1, MR1, FcRn, and HFE — all TM-anchored surface proteins — broadening its surface presence further. There is a known equilibrium exchange with a circulating soluble pool, but this does not displace the dominant, constitutive, ubiquitous cell-surface-resident state. Anti-B2M antibody therapeutic programs explicitly target the cell-surface form. Verdict: yes / stable_complex_partner.
